Dear Mr. Hallibrin:
Susan Barbrow lrom your otfice suggesled I write to you about a concem I have. lt is aboul the facl that
during a Board relreat which included members ol the board, administration and representatives ,rom the
medical stafl ol the Eden Township Hospital District, plans were made lo insure lhe re-eleclion of the three
incumbenls who will be running again in November 1990. The memorandum which I requested and then
purchased from lhe district, is a xeroxed copy oI a summary ol disqJssions held during a retreat held in
February 1990 by the board. I have enclosed a copy which relers lo my concern.
Eden Township Hospital District is a notlor-profit district hospital which, as I undersland it, does not pay
taxes and does receive some monies lrom county and slale agencies. lt has an elected board. I believe
these descriplors indicale that the Board o, Directors and stall come under the provisions of Proposition
73 which restricts the use ol district stalf tirne and materials lo assisl in the re-election of incumbents. As
you will see lrom lhe retreat report, plans were made lor hospital staff members to write speeches,
publicize and inlluence the public in behal, ol lhe re-election of lhe lhree incumbenls. Wouldn't this be a
violation of Ptop. 73?
I also have a copy ot a "Report to the Community'lor 1989 which was mass mailed and it contains a picture
of lhe live board menbers. As I understand il, this is also in violation ol Prop. 73. The hospital Foundation
also mails oul a newslener and I noticed thal piclures ol board members are also used in this publication. I
am not sure if the Foundation publication lalls under lhe sarne rules.
lamplanningto runrorasealonthe hospital board o, directors in November 1990. lhave liled, this date,
FPPC lorm 501 . lt would appear lrom the above, lhat lhe incumbents have an unlair and possibly illegal
advantage il they can use hospital slat time and malerials to enhance lheir campaigns.
At this time, I am not liling a complaint. This is a request lor inrormalion and "enlightenment." lf you have
any questions, please call me (415) 8A7 -0152 X209 (work) or (415) 538-9678 (home).
Sincerely,

sections.question: Wouldn't this be a violation of Prop. 73? I also have a copy of a "Report to the Community" for 1989 which was mass mailed and it contains a picture of the five board members. As I understand it, this is also in violation of Prop. 73. The hospital Foundation also mails out a newsletter and I noticed that pictures of board members are also used in this publication. I am not sure if the Foundation publication falls under the same rules.
sections.question_synthetic: The sender asks whether Eden Township Hospital District's use of district staff time and materials to assist in re-electing incumbent board members, and the district's mass mailing of materials featuring incumbent board members, violates Proposition 73's restrictions on public agency resources being used for political campaigns.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. The document includes two main parts: (1) the inquiry letter from Suzanne Barba dated June 15, 1990, and (2) attached retreat summary documents from February 1990. No FPPC conclusion/advice section exists in this document. The sender explicitly states 'At this time, I am not filing a complaint. This is a request for information and enlightenment.' High confidence in question extraction; lower confidence due to lack of formal section headers and presence of attached materials that provide context.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Wouldn't this be a violation of Prop. 73? I also have a copy of a "Report to the Community" for 1989 which was mass mailed and it contains a picture of the five board members. As I understand it, this is also in violation of Prop. 73. The hospital Foundation also mails out a newsletter and I noticed that pictures of board members are also used in this publication. I am not sure if the Foundation publication falls under the same rules.

embedding.summary: A prospective board candidate for Eden Township Hospital District requests FPPC guidance on whether the district violated Proposition 73 by using staff time and materials during a February 1990 board retreat to plan the re-election of three incumbent board members, and by featuring board members in a mass-mailed community report and foundation newsletter.
